name: Greek Mythology
description: Test your fundamental knowledge of the primary gods and essential myths of ancient Greece.

questions.question: Who is the ruler of the Olympian gods and the god of the sky?
questions.explain: Zeus is the supreme deity in Greek mythology who rules from Mount Olympus.

questions.question: Which Greek goddess is associated with wisdom, warfare, and handicraft?
questions.explain: Athena is the patron goddess of Athens, representing wisdom and strategic battle.

questions.question: Who is the god of the sea and earthquakes, known for carrying a trident?
questions.explain: Poseidon controls the oceans and is symbolized by his powerful three-pronged spear.

questions.question: Which twin deity is the goddess of the hunt, wild animals, and the moon?
questions.explain: Artemis is the daughter of Zeus and Leto, and the twin sister of Apollo.

questions.question: Who is the ruler of the underworld and the dead in Greek myths?
questions.explain: Hades rules the subterranean realm of the dead which also bears his name.

questions.question: What is the name of the winged horse born from the blood of Medusa?
questions.explain: Pegasus is the famous stallion capable of flight captured by Bellerophon.

questions.question: Which Greek hero performed twelve legendary labors as penance?
questions.explain: Heracles carried out the twelve labors under the orders of King Eurystheus.

questions.question: Who is the goddess of love, beauty, and desire, born from sea foam?
questions.explain: Aphrodite represents love and physical beauty, married to Hephaestus.

questions.question: What creature has the head of a bull and the body of a man?
questions.explain: The Minotaur lived inside the Labyrinth designed by Daedalus in Crete.

questions.question: Who is the messenger of the gods, recognizable by his winged sandals?
questions.explain: Hermes guides souls to the underworld and delivers divine messages swiftly.
